task_completion.description: Verify the segmentation threshold is set to target soft tissue (approximately 0 to 100 HU).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The thresholding panel in the software shows the minimum value set near 0 and the maximum value set near 100.

task_completion.description: Verify a segmentation mask is created based on the soft-tissue threshold.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D slice views (axial, coronal, sagittal) display a colored overlay highlighting the soft tissue areas of the scan.

task_completion.description: Verify the intracranial space (brain) is isolated from the external scalp and soft tissue.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The colored mask overlay in the 2D slices is restricted to the inner skull cavity; the external soft tissue (scalp/skin) is erased, deselected, or disconnected from the brain mask.

task_completion.description: Verify a 3D surface model representing only the intracranial space is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer panel displays a solid 3D model resembling a brain (endocast). It should not show external facial features, the outer scalp, or the full skull.

task_completion.description: Verify the resulting 3D surface is exported as a binary STL file to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export' or 'Save As' dialog box shows the file being saved as 'brain_model.stl' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory, or a file manager/terminal confirms the file's creation at this exact path.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the medical imaging software's GUI to perform the segmentation and 3D modeling.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the software's interface (e.g., threshold tools, mask editing, 3D surface creation) rather than running a script in the terminal to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actively isolated the brain cavity rather than exporting the unedited soft-tissue mask.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visible use of mask editing tools (eraser, brush, region growing, or keeping largest connected component) in the GUI to remove the scalp.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the software's export functionality to create the STL file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The software's 'Export 3D surface' dialog is visible, ensuring the file wasn't just mocked by typing 'touch /home/ga/Documents/brain_model.stl' in a terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses InVesalius 3.0 medical imaging software with a real CT cranium dataset (case 0051). A correct endocast (brain cavity model) for this normal adult scan should have a volume of approximately 1106 mL to 1700 mL. The VLM should look for InVesalius UI elements for thresholding, mask editing, and 3D surface generation.
